MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ana T. Portillo, Esq. of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party DAP, Inc., erronously sued herein as DAP Products, Inc.
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Carl Wagner, et al.

RULING:
Defendant DAP, Inc., erronously sued herein as DAP Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant DAP, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence and strict liability) and 3 (loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issues 2 (false representation) and 4 (punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Wagner was exposed to asbestos-containing DAP 33 glazing compound. Mr. Wagner's deposition testimony and defendant's GO 129 interrogatory responses preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden of production, the declaration of Mr. Wagner creates a triable fact regarding his exposure to an asbestos-containing product. The objections to Mr. Wagner's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
